expectations.text: A report.md file was created in the outputs directory
Report correctly identifies this as a Ruby on Rails application
expectations.evidence: 'Rails 7.2, React + TypeScript (migrating from Angular)' with full scc breakdown

expectations.text: Report contains findings from multiple distinct analysis dimensions
expectations.evidence: 8 findings across Code Health (StinkScores), Reliability (swallowed errors), Security (SQL injection), Maintainability (lib dirs, Angular), State (callbacks), Architecture (routes)

expectations.text: Report includes a 'what's working well' section
expectations.evidence: 'What's Working Well' with 5 items: clear arch direction, feature-organized frontend, mature dev tooling, strong Composer architecture, active modernization

expectations.text: Findings reference specific file paths and line numbers
expectations.evidence: StinkScore table with exact scores, '637 lines, 84 changes in 6 months', brakeman line refs, jscpd clone counts (158 JS/TS, 61 Ruby lib, 24 Composer)

expectations.text: Report includes git churn or change frequency data
expectations.evidence: '84 changes in 6 months' for routes, '80 changes' for types.ts, skunk ChurnxCost values (108,685 for meetings_controller)

expectations.text: Findings are ranked or prioritized with severity levels
expectations.evidence: Numbered 1-8 'ranked by impact', high/medium severity labels, Lower Priority section

expectations.text: Report notes when multiple agents flagged the same area
expectations.evidence: 'Flagged by: Complexity & Churn, Coupling, Structure' on god models, convergence noted throughout

expectations.text: At least 3 findings include concrete improvement directions
expectations.evidence: All 8 findings have Direction fields with specific actions
